title: Web: extract renderer-registry abstraction from DocumentViewer
description:
Refactor web/src/components/Document/DocumentViewer.tsx so the content renderer is chosen via a registry instead of hardcoded ReactMarkdown. Part of story abfd6016. Unblocks task eeafaa78 (HtmlRenderer).

## Implementation

1. New DocumentRenderer interface: { id: string; match(content, ref): boolean; Component: FC<DocumentRendererProps> }.
2. New registry at web/src/components/Document/renderers/index.ts. Ordered; first-match-wins; catch-all MarkdownRenderer last. Exports pickRenderer(content, ref?) helper.
3. Extract current ReactMarkdown body (code-block handler, syntax highlighting, search highlighting effect, plugins, the ReactMarkdown call itself) verbatim into renderers/MarkdownRenderer.tsx. Zero behavior change for existing markdown docs.
4. Stub renderers/HtmlRenderer.tsx with a placeholder component (real implementation lands in task eeafaa78) so the registry can select it by content_type === "text/html".
5. DocumentViewer.tsx becomes a thin shell: fetch as today; keep header/footer/history-panel scaffolding; delegate content body to the matched renderer.
6. Unit tests (Vitest) covering dispatch behavior.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] web/src/components/Document/renderers/index.ts exports rendererRegistry (array of DocumentRenderer) and pickRenderer(content, ref?) helper.
- [ ] pickRenderer({content_type: "text/html", ...}) returns the html renderer entry — covered by a unit test.
- [ ] pickRenderer({content_type: "text/markdown", ...}) returns the markdown renderer entry — covered by a unit test.
- [ ] pickRenderer({content_type: "application/unknown", ...}) returns the markdown renderer entry (fallback) — covered by a unit test.
- [ ] DocumentViewer.tsx contains no direct `from 'react-markdown'` import — markdown rendering is entirely inside MarkdownRenderer.
- [ ] DocumentViewer.tsx still fetches content via apiClient.getDocumentContent and still renders the header/footer/history scaffolding — existing DocumentViewer tests (if any) continue to pass.
- [ ] An existing markdown-linked issue rendered through DocumentViewer produces the same DOM structure for heading, code-block, and Mermaid elements as before — covered by a smoke test rendering a fixture and checking for expected elements.
- [ ] cd web && npm test -- --run passes; npm run lint is clean; npm run build succeeds.
- [ ] cargo-ci gate passes (no Rust changes expected; gate succeeds trivially).
- [ ] code-review gate passes.
